Petition To Establish Estate?S Claim Of Ownership To Property And Order Transferring Said Property To Decedent?S Estate (Prob. Code 850(A)(2)(C)
Off Calendar Jan-25-2023 Continued To Feb-15-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom: Nicole C. Kelly, Esq. for Evelia Rojas Leon (Petitioner/Administrator). Thomas Smurro, Esq. for San Francisco Eagle Bar, LLC. Alejandro Montiel (Respondent).

Ms. Kelly provided extensive argument in support of the petition to establish the estate's claim of ownership stating that this is bad faith by the respondent who is acting in concert with the attorney on behalf of the LLC, that there isn't informed written consent to act, and that if this matter isn't settled today that a receiver needs to be appointed. Mr. Smurro stated that he represents the San Francisco Legal Bar, LLC, that there are procedural issues, and that the Court should not consider the late-filed reply brief or he should be allowed to reply. At this point in the proceedings, Mr. Montiel noted his presence on Zoom. Mr. Smurro provided a brief background stating that the first LLC was discontinued, that an "Inc." was set up to save taxes but they could not obtain a liquor license, that the CPA then tried to "re-up" the LLC, and after that Mr. Montiel was told to start a new LLC with the same name. Ms. Kelly addressed the late reply brief. Mr. Smurro requested that he be allowed to reply/given time to respond and stated that they can then go forward on an 850 petition. Mr. Smurro briefly addressed the 50/50 ownership, provided a brief background re: the operating agreement noting that Mr. Montiel contacted Ms. Kelly about the issue, and asked that the Court ask the estate to cooperate with the other owner to at least initiate the operating agreement. The Court addressed Ms. Kelly stating that the claims that are being made are more of a civil action. Ms. Kelly stated that at a minimum that she wanted an order to transfer 50% of the property to the estate, noted that there are so many subsequent entities, stated that there are three entities with 50% ownership, and that what she would like to see is an order that defines what interests are being talked about. Additional argument is held.

At the conclusion of argument, the Petition to Establish Estate's Claim of Ownership to Property and Order Transferring Said Property to Decedent's Estate is continued to February 15, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. A response to the late reply is to be filed by February 1, 2023. No sur-reply.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Choi. Matter not reported.
